{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].

==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].

{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].

{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.

The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].

{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].

{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].

{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].
