{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Miyakozuka Kofun}} *[https://www.asukamura.jp/files/bunkazai_bunkazai/20.pdf Asuka Village home page]
Miyakozuka Kofun

The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.

The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.

{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Miyakozuka Kofun}} *[https://www.asukamura.jp/files/bunkazai_bunkazai/20.pdf Asuka Village home page]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Miyakozuka Kofun}} *[https://www.asukamura.jp/files/bunkazai_bunkazai/20.pdf Asuka Village home page]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Miyakozuka Kofun}} *[https://www.asukamura.jp/files/bunkazai_bunkazai/20.pdf Asuka Village home page]
Miyakozuka Kofun

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[:en:Asuka Station|Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Miyakozuka Kofun}} *[https://www.asukamura.jp/files/bunkazai_bunkazai/20.pdf Asuka Village home page]

{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[Goguryeo]] and [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[granite]] [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[:en:Asuka Station|Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] and [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[:en:Granite|Granite]] [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[:en:Asuka Station|Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Miyakozuka Kofun}} *[https://www.asukamura.jp/files/bunkazai_bunkazai/20.pdf Asuka Village home page]

It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[:en:Asuka Station|Asuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].

{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] and [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[:en:Granite|Granite]] [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been lost due to [[tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[:en:Asuka Station|Asuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[:en:Step pyramid|Step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] and [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[:en:Granite|Granite]] [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the stone coffin, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been lost due to [[:en:tomb robbery|tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[:en:Asuka Station|Asuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Yoshino Line|Yoshino Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Miyakozuka Kofun}} *[https://www.asukamura.jp/files/bunkazai_bunkazai/20.pdf Asuka Village home page]

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[:en:Step pyramid|Step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] and [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[:en:Granite|Granite]] [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the Sarcophagus, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been lost due to [[:en:tomb robbery|tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[:en:Asuka Station|Asuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Yoshino Line|Yoshino Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] (d.570).

The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Miyakozuka Kofun]][[en:Miyakozuka Kofun]][[ja:都塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24869088]] Wikidata name: Miyakozuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.463636|N|135.827694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[:en:Step pyramid|Step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] and [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[:en:Granite|Granite]] [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the Sarcophagus, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been lost due to [[:en:tomb robbery|tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[:en:Asuka Station|Asuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Yoshino Line|Yoshino Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Miyakozuka Kofun}} *[https://www.asukamura.jp/files/bunkazai_bunkazai/20.pdf Asuka Village home page]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Miyakozuka Kofun]][[en:Miyakozuka Kofun]][[ja:都塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24869088]] Wikidata name: Miyakozuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.463636|N|135.827694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[:en:Step pyramid|Step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] and [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[:en:Granite|Granite]] [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the Sarcophagus, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been lost due to [[:en:tomb robbery|tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[:en:Asuka Station|Asuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Yoshino Line|Yoshino Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Miyakozuka Kofun}} *[https://www.asukamura.jp/files/bunkazai_bunkazai/20.pdf Asuka Village home page]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Miyakozuka Kofun]][[en:Miyakozuka Kofun]][[ja:都塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24869088]] Wikidata name: Miyakozuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.463636|N|135.827694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[:en:Step pyramid|Step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] and [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[:en:Granite|Granite]] [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the Sarcophagus, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been lost due to [[:en:tomb robbery|tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[:en:Asuka Station|Asuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Yoshino Line|Yoshino Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[:en:Step pyramid|Step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] and [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[:en:Granite|Granite]] [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the Sarcophagus, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been lost due to [[:en:tomb robbery|tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[:en:Asuka Station|Asuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Yoshino Line|Yoshino Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Miyakozuka Kofun]][[en:Miyakozuka Kofun]][[ja:都塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Miyakozuka Kofun]][[en:Miyakozuka Kofun]][[ja:都塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Miyakozuka Kofun]][[en:Miyakozuka Kofun]][[ja:都塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24869088]] Wikidata name: Miyakozuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.463636|N|135.827694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[:en:Step pyramid|Step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] and [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[:en:Granite|Granite]] [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the Sarcophagus, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been lost due to [[:en:tomb robbery|tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[:en:Asuka Station|Asuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Yoshino Line|Yoshino Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Miyakozuka Kofun]][[en:Miyakozuka Kofun]][[ja:都塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Miyakozuka Kofun]][[en:Miyakozuka Kofun]][[ja:都塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Miyakozuka Kofun]][[en:Miyakozuka Kofun]][[ja:都塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24869088]] Wikidata name: Miyakozuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Miyakozuka Kofun | native_name =都塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Miyakozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyakozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.463636|N|135.827694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|49.09|N|135|49|39.7|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Miyakozuka Kofun'''|都塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakata neighborhood of the village of [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003995|title= 都塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Kinchozuka Kofun'''|金鳥塚|}} and is believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] (d.570).
==Overview== The Miyakozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located located on a ridge about 400 meters south-southeast of [[Ishibutai Kofun]] on the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] in 1967 and from 2013 to 2014. The tumulus has a restored size of 41 meters east–west, 42 meters north–south, and over 4.5 meters high. The surface of the mound is thought to have been built in a stepped pattern, with at least four steps, each 0.3-0.6 meters high. This "[[:en:Step pyramid|Step pyramid]]" structure has been linked to stepped stone mounds found in [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] and [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] around the 5th century. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat measuring 1.0-1.5 meters wide and 0.4 meters deep. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type [[:en:Granite|Granite]] [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with an opening to the southwest. The chamber is about 12 meters long. A hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] with six rope hanging protrusions was placed inside the chamber. There are also traces of a secondary burial with a wooden coffin was placed on the floor in front of the Sarcophagus, based on traces of nails and stones. Most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been lost due to [[:en:tomb robbery|tomb robbery]] as the entrance is known to have been open since at least the middle of theb [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]; however, some shards of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery and iron products such as knives, iron arrowheads, and iron nails have been found.
<gallery> Miyakozuka-kofun sekikan.jpg|Burial chamber Miyakozuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus </gallery>
The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of [[:en:Asuka Station|Asuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Yoshino Line|Yoshino Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Miyakozuka Kofun}} *[https://www.asukamura.jp/files/bunkazai_bunkazai/20.pdf Asuka Village home page]

The tumulus is thought to have been built in the late 6th century, during the Late Kofun period. It is located in an area known have been a stronghold of the [[Soga clan]] during the Asuka period, and it is believed to have slightly predate the Ishibutai Kofun, which may have originally had the same structure before its earthen tumulus was lost.
The tumulus is about 3.8 kilometers east of {{ill|Asuka Station|en|Asuka Station|WD=Q4811860|lt=Asuka Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Yoshino Line|en|Yoshino Line|WD=Q901085|lt=Yoshino Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Miyakozuka Kofun}} *[https://www.asukamura.jp/files/bunkazai_bunkazai/20.pdf Asuka Village home page]
